Architecture.description: Integer representing the CPU architecture exposed by the browser.

Audio.description: AudioContext fingerprint or negative status when unavailable. The negative statuses can be: - -1: A permanent status for those browsers which are known to always suspend audio context - -2: A permanent status for browsers that don't support the signal - -3: A temporary status that means that an unexpected timeout has happened

category.description: The type and purpose of the bot.

confidence.description: Confidence level of the bot identification.

identity.description:
The verification status of the bot's identity:
 * `verified` - well-known bot with publicly verifiable identity, directed by the bot provider.
 * `signed` - bot that signs its platform via Web Bot Auth, directed by the bot provider’s customers.
 * `spoofed` - bot that claims a public identity but fails verification.
 * `unknown` - bot that does not publish a verifiable identity.

name.description: The specific name or identifier of the bot.

provider.description: The organization or company operating the bot.

provider_url.description: The URL of the bot provider's website.

BotResult.description:
Bot detection result:
 * `bad` - bad bot detected, such as Selenium, Puppeteer, Playwright, headless browsers, and so on
 * `good` - good bot detected, such as Google bot, Baidu Spider, AlexaBot and so on
 * `not_detected` - the visitor is not a bot

BotType.description: Additional classification of the bot type if detected.

BundleId.description: Bundle Id of the iOS application integrated with the Fingerprint SDK for the event. For example: `com.foo.app`

Canvas.description: Canvas fingerprint containing winding flag plus geometry/text hashes.

geometry.description: Hash of geometry rendering output or `unsupported` markers.

text.description: Hash of text rendering output or `unsupported` markers.

ClientReferrer.description: Client Referrer field corresponds to the `document.referrer` field gathered during an identification request. The value is an empty string if the user navigated to the page directly (not through a link, but, for example, by using a bookmark) For example: `https://example.com/blog/my-article`

ClonedApp.description: Android specific cloned application detection. There are 2 values:  * `true` - Presence of app cloners work detected (e.g. fully cloned application found or launch of it inside of a not main working profile detected). * `false` - No signs of cloned application detected or the client is not Android.

CookiesEnabled.description: Whether the cookies are enabled in the browser.

DateTimeLocale.description: Locale derived from the Intl.DateTimeFormat API. Negative values indicate known error states. The negative statuses can be: - "-1": A permanent status for browsers that don't support Intl API. - "-2": A permanent status for browsers that don't supportDateTimeFormat constructor. - "-3": A permanent status for browsers in which DateTimeFormat locale is undefined or null.

DeveloperTools.description: `true` if the browser is Chrome with DevTools open or Firefox with Developer Tools open, `false` otherwise.

DeviceMemory.description: Rounded amount of RAM (in gigabytes) reported by the browser.

Emoji.description: Bounding box metrics describing how the emoji glyph renders.

font.description: Font family reported by the browser when drawing the emoji.

Emulator.description:
Android specific emulator detection. There are 2 values: 
* `true` - Emulated environment detected (e.g. launch inside of AVD). 
* `false` - No signs of emulated environment detected or the client is not Android.

EnvironmentId.description: Environment Id of the event. For example: `ae_47abaca3db2c7c43`

Event.description: Contains results from Fingerprint Identification and all active Smart Signals.

EventId.description: Unique identifier of the user's request. The first portion of the event_id is a unix epoch milliseconds timestamp For example: `1758130560902.8tRtrH`

EventRuleAction.description: Describes the action the client should take, according to the rule in the ruleset that matched the event. When getting an event by event ID, the rule_action will only be included when the ruleset_id query parameter is specified.

EventRuleActionAllow.description: Informs the client that the request should be forwarded to the origin with optional request header modifications.

EventRuleActionBlock.description: Informs the client the request should be blocked using the response described by this rule action.

headers.description: A list of headers to send.

EventSearch.description: Contains a list of all identification events matching the specified search criteria.

pagination_key.description: Use this value in the `pagination_key` parameter to request the next page of search results.

total_hits.description: This value represents the total number of events matching the search query, up to the limit provided in the `total_hits` query parameter. Only present if the `total_hits` query parameter was provided.

FactoryReset.description: The time of the most recent factory reset that happened on the **mobile device** is expressed as Unix epoch time. When a factory reset cannot be detected on the mobile device or when the request is initiated from a browser,  this field will correspond to the *epoch* time (i.e 1 Jan 1970 UTC) as a value of 0. See [Factory Reset Detection](https://docs.fingerprint.com/docs/smart-signals-reference#factory-reset-detection) to learn more about this Smart Signal.

FontPreferences.description: Baseline measurement of canonical fonts rendered on the device. Numeric width metrics, in CSS pixels, for the canonical fonts collected by the agent.

Fonts.description: List of fonts detected on the device.

Frida.description:
[Frida](https://frida.re/docs/) detection for Android and iOS devices. There are 2 values:
* `true` - Frida detected
* `false` - No signs of Frida or the client is not a mobile device.

accuracy_radius.description: The IP address is likely to be within this radius (in km) of the specified location.

HardwareConcurrency.description: Number of logical CPU cores reported by the browser.

HighActivity.description: Flag indicating if the request came from a high-activity visitor.

attack_source.description: IP address was part of a known network attack (SSH/HTTPS).

email_spam.description: IP address was part of a known email spam attack (SMTP).

tor_node.description: IP address was part of known TOR network activity.

IPInfo.description: Details about the request IP address. Has separate fields for v4 and v6 IP address versions.

first_seen_at.description: Unix epoch time milliseconds timestamp indicating the time at which this visitor ID was first seen. example: `1758069706642` - Corresponding to Wed Sep 17 2025 00:41:46 GMT+0000

last_seen_at.description: Unix epoch time milliseconds timestamp indicating the time at which this visitor ID was last seen. example: `1758069706642` - Corresponding to Wed Sep 17 2025 00:41:46 GMT+0000

visitor_found.description: Attribute represents if a visitor had been identified before.

visitor_id.description: String of 20 characters that uniquely identifies the visitor's browser or mobile device.

score.description: The confidence score is a floating-point number between 0 and 1 that represents the probability of accurate identification.

version.description: The version name of the method used to calculate the Confidence score. This field is only present for customers who opted in to an alternative calculation method.

Incognito.description: `true` if we detected incognito mode used in the browser, `false` otherwise.

name.description: The name of the specific integration, e.g. "fingerprint-pro-react".
The name of the specific subintegration, e.g. "preact".

version.description: The version of the specific subintegration, e.g. "10.21.0".
The version of the specific integration, e.g. "3.11.10".

IpAddress.description: IP address of the requesting browser or bot.

Jailbroken.description:
iOS specific jailbreak detection. There are 2 values: 
* `true` - Jailbreak detected.
* `false` - No signs of jailbreak or the client is not iOS.

Languages.description: Navigator languages reported by the agent including fallbacks. Each inner array represents ordered language preferences reported by different APIs.

LinkedId.description: A customer-provided id that was sent with the request.

LocationSpoofing.description: Flag indicating whether the request came from a mobile device with location spoofing enabled.

Math.description: Hash of Math APIs used for entropy collection.

MitMAttack.description:
* `true` - When requests made from your users' mobile devices to Fingerprint servers have been intercepted and potentially modified. 
* `false` - Otherwise or when the request originated from a browser.
See [MitM Attack Detection](https://docs.fingerprint.com/docs/smart-signals-reference#mitm-attack-detection) to learn more about this Smart Signal.

PackageName.description: Package name of the Android application integrated with the Fingerprint SDK for the event. For example: `com.foo.app`

PrivacySettings.description: `true` if the request is from a privacy aware browser (e.g. Tor) or from a browser in which fingerprinting is blocked. Otherwise `false`.

Proximity.description: Proximity ID represents a fixed geographical zone in a discrete global grid within which the device is observed.

confidence.description:
A value between `0` and `1` representing the likelihood that the true device location lies within the mapped proximity zone.
  * Scores closer to `1` indicate high confidence that the location is inside the mapped proximity zone.
  * Scores closer to `0` indicate lower confidence, suggesting the true location may fall in an adjacent zone.

id.description: A stable privacy-preserving identifier for a given proximity zone.

precision_radius.description: The radius of the proximity zone’s precision level, in meters.

Proxy.description: IP address was used by a public proxy provider or belonged to a known recent residential proxy

ProxyConfidence.description: Confidence level of the proxy detection. If a proxy is not detected, confidence is "high". If it's detected, can be "low", "medium", or "high".

last_seen_at.description: Unix millisecond timestamp with hourly resolution of when this IP was last seen as a proxy

provider.description:
String representing the last proxy service provider detected when this
IP was synced. An IP can be shared by multiple service providers.

proxy_type.description:
Residential proxies use real user IP addresses to appear as legitimate traffic, 
while data center proxies are public proxies hosted in data centers

RawDeviceAttributes.description: A curated subset of raw browser/device attributes that the API surface exposes. Each property contains a value or object with the data for the collected signal.

Replayed.description: `true` if we determined that this payload was replayed, `false` otherwise.

RequestHeaderModifications.description: The set of header modifications to apply, in the following order: remove, set, append.

append.description: The list of headers to append.

remove.description: The list of headers to remove.

set.description: The list of headers to set, overwriting any existing headers with the same name.

RootApps.description:
Android specific root management apps detection. There are 2 values: 
* `true` - Root Management Apps detected (e.g. Magisk).
* `false` - No Root Management Apps detected or the client isn't Android.

RuleActionBody.description: The response body to send to the client.

value.description: The value of the header field.

RuleActionType.description: Describes the action to take with the request.

RuleExpression.description: The expression of the rule that matched the identification event.

RuleId.description: The ID of the rule that matched the identification event.

RulesetId.description: The ID of the evaluated ruleset.

SDK.description: Contains information about the SDK used to perform the request.

platform.description: Platform of the SDK used for the identification request.

version.description: Version string of the SDK used for the identification request. For example: `"3.12.1"`

SupplementaryIDHighRecall.description: A supplementary browser identifier that prioritizes coverage over precision. The High Recall ID algorithm matches more generously, i.e., this identifier will remain the same even when there are subtle differences between two requests. This algorithm does not create as many new visitor IDs as the standard algorithms do, but there could be an increase in false-positive identification.

first_seen_at.description: Unix epoch time milliseconds timestamp indicating the time at which this ID was first seen. example: `1758069706642` - Corresponding to Wed Sep 17 2025 00:41:46 GMT+0000

last_seen_at.description: Unix epoch time milliseconds timestamp indicating the time at which this ID was last seen. example: `1758069706642` - Corresponding to Wed Sep 17 2025 00:41:46 GMT+0000

visitor_found.description: Attribute represents if a visitor had been identified before.

visitor_id.description: String of 20 characters that uniquely identifies the visitor's browser or mobile device.

Suspect.description: Field is `true` if you have previously set the `suspect` flag for this event using the [Server API Update event endpoint](https://docs.fingerprint.com/reference/server-api-v4-update-event).

SuspectScore.description: Suspect Score is an easy way to integrate Smart Signals into your fraud protection work flow.  It is a weighted representation of all Smart Signals present in the payload that helps identify suspicious activity. The value range is [0; S] where S is sum of all Smart Signals weights.  See more details here: https://docs.fingerprint.com/docs/suspect-score

Tags.description: A customer-provided value or an object that was sent with the identification request or updated later.

Tampering.description:
Flag indicating browser tampering was detected. This happens when either:
  * There are inconsistencies in the browser configuration that cross internal tampering thresholds (see `tampering_details.anomaly_score`).
  * The browser signature resembles an "anti-detect" browser specifically designed to evade fingerprinting (see `tampering_details.anti_detect_browser`).

anomaly_score.description:
Confidence score (`0.0 - 1.0`) for tampering detection:
  * Values above `0.5` indicate tampering.
  * Values below `0.5` indicate genuine browsers.

anti_detect_browser.description: True if the identified browser resembles an "anti-detect" browser, such as Incognition, which attempts to evade identification by manipulating its fingerprint.

Timestamp.description: Timestamp of the event with millisecond precision in Unix time.

Timezone.description: Timezone identifier detected on the client.

Url.description: Page URL from which the request was sent. For example `https://example.com/`

UserAgent.description: User Agent of the client, for example: `Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; Win64; x64) ....`

Velocity.description:
Sums key data points for a specific `visitor_id`, `ip_address` and `linked_id` at three distinct time
intervals: 5 minutes, 1 hour, and 24 hours as follows: 

- Number of distinct IP addresses associated to the visitor Id.
- Number of distinct linked Ids associated with the visitor Id.
- Number of distinct countries associated with the visitor Id.
- Number of identification events associated with the visitor Id.
- Number of identification events associated with the detected IP address.
- Number of distinct IP addresses associated with the provided linked Id.
- Number of distinct visitor Ids associated with the provided linked Id.

The `24h` interval of `distinct_ip`, `distinct_linked_id`, `distinct_country`,
`distinct_ip_by_linked_id` and `distinct_visitor_id_by_linked_id` will be omitted 
if the number of `events` for the visitor Id in the last 24
hours (`events.['24h']`) is higher than 20.000.

All will not necessarily be returned in a response, some may be omitted if the 
associated event does not have the required data, such as a linked_id.

VelocityData.description: Is absent if the velocity data could not be generated for the visitor Id.

1_hour.description: Count for the last 1 hour of velocity data, from the time of the event.

24_hours.description: The `24_hours` interval of `distinct_ip`, `distinct_linked_id`, `distinct_country`, `distinct_ip_by_linked_id` and `distinct_visitor_id_by_linked_id` will be omitted if the number of `events` for the visitor Id in the last 24 hours (`events.['24_hours']`) is higher than 20.000.

5_minutes.description: Count for the last 5 minutes of velocity data, from the time of the event.

VirtualMachine.description: `true` if the request came from a browser running inside a virtual machine (e.g. VMWare), `false` otherwise.

Vpn.description: VPN or other anonymizing service has been used when sending the request.

VpnConfidence.description: A confidence rating for the VPN detection result — "low", "medium", or "high". Depends on the combination of results returned from all VPN detection methods.

auxiliary_mobile.description: This method applies to mobile devices only. Indicates the result of additional methods used to detect a VPN in mobile devices.

os_mismatch.description: The browser runs on a different operating system than the operating system inferred from the request network signature.

public_vpn.description: Request IP address is owned and used by a public VPN service provider.

relay.description:
Request IP address belongs to a relay service provider, indicating the use of relay services like [Apple Private relay](https://support.apple.com/en-us/102602) or [Cloudflare Warp](https://developers.cloudflare.com/warp-client/).

* Like VPNs, relay services anonymize the visitor's true IP address.
* Unlike traditional VPNs, relay services don't let visitors spoof their location by choosing an exit node in a different country.

This field allows you to differentiate VPN users and relay service users in your fraud prevention logic.

timezone_mismatch.description: The browser timezone doesn't match the timezone inferred from the request IP address.

VpnOriginCountry.description: Country of the request (only for Android SDK version >= 2.4.0, ISO 3166 format or unknown).

VpnOriginTimezone.description: Local timezone which is used in timezone_mismatch method.

WebGlBasics.description: Render and vendor strings reported by the WebGL context.

WebGlExtensions.description: Hashes of WebGL context attributes and extension support.
